steps.title: Use the scatterplot trend
steps.content: The scatterplot decreases as $x$ increases, so the model needs a negative slope. This eliminates choices A and C immediately because their slopes are positive.

steps.title: Estimate intercept and slope
steps.content: The left side of the scatterplot is near $y=10$ when $x$ is near $0$, so the $y$-intercept should be around $9$ or $10$. The right side is near $y=1$ or $2$ when $x$ is near $9$, so the slope is about $$\frac{2-10}{9-0}\approx -0.9.$$

steps.title: Overlay the answer choices
steps.content: Graphing representative points with the candidate from choice D makes the fit clear. The line has intercept $9.4$ and slope $-0.9$, matching the scatterplot. The other choices are already eliminated by slope sign or unreasonable intercept.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B has a negative slope, but its intercept is only $0.9$ and its slope $-9.4$ is much too steep. Choice D is the only line with both the right intercept and the right negative slope, so D is correct.
